titleStmt.title: No. 97The Ambassador in Burma (Sebald) to the Department of State

div.head:
No. 97
The Ambassador in Burma (Sebald) to the Department of State
Repeated to Bangkok and Taipei.
div.p: 234. Gist of conversation with Kyaw Nyein: Series of conferences among government leaders have led to conclusions.

list.item: Chinese Nationalist Government has no intention bringing about repatriation KMT troops;
Committee has become device for delay and lip service to United Nations resolution;
Chiang Kai-shek has given no indication sincerity or desire help Burma solve problem;

In view flouting United Nations by Chinese Government and apparent inability Burma interest great powers come to Burma’s assistance against aggression leaders seriously debated pros and cons dramatic withdrawal Burma from United Nations with purpose high-lighting KMT problem (no decision reached this point mytel 229);Dated Sept. 12; the Embassy reported that it had learned that some Burmese Government leaders were pressing for action, including possible withdrawal from the United Nations, to indicate Burma’s dissatisfaction with the lack of results from the Bangkok talks. (690B.9321/9–1253)

Appreciate difficulties United States taking drastic action against Chinese Nationalist Government in UN but anticipate considerable support should Burma decide request branding Taipei as aggressor. (Myint Thein apparently not yet instructed pending outcome joint committee deliberations.)

div.p: I appropriately countered each of above points and counseled further patience and faith in joint committee which has almost reached agreement. Expressed hope Burma would not compromise committee’s efforts. Kyaw Nyein said Burma has right to know whether Chinese Government (a) intends withdraw KMT troops (b) how many (c) when. Unless these questions answered immediately no point continuing committee discussions.
